title: Pilot ingestion: EPA ECHO/SDWIS for one small region — provenance-stamped, checksummed snapshot
context: The pilot ingestion is the first end-to-end test of the source-registry gate, normalized schema, and snapshot reproducibility before broader coverage is attempted. It targets one small region using EPA ECHO/SDWIS (US federal public-domain data), which is the primary compliance and violation database for the Safe Drinking Water Act. Any API key required is a CI/pipeline secret only — never written to logs, snapshots, or commits. The adapter must be read-only and snapshot raw responses before any transform so the raw→normalized pipeline is auditable.
objective: Implement a read-only ingestion adapter for EPA ECHO/SDWIS covering one small pilot region that: (1) passes the source-registry/license gate; (2) snapshots raw API responses with retrieval metadata before any transform; (3) normalizes raw data to the wqe-data-003 schema deterministically (same source vintage + pipeline version yields same snapshot checksum); (4) produces a provenance-stamped, checksummed snapshot that reconciles to source in CI; (5) uses any API key only via CI/pipeline secrets, never writing it to logs, snapshots, or commits.
acceptanceCriteria: Read-only adapter snapshots raw EPA ECHO/SDWIS responses with retrieval metadata (URL, timestamp, HTTP status) before any transform
Raw-to-normalized transform is deterministic: same source vintage + same pipeline version produces the same snapshot checksum
Output snapshot reconciles to the source data in a CI job; any reconciliation failure blocks merge
Adapter passes the source-registry license gate; EPA SDWIS is registered with public-domain license and verification note
Any API key is accessed only via CI/pipeline secret store; no key appears in logs, snapshot files, or committed code
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass
output: PR adding pipeline/adapters/epa-echo/ adapter, a pilot snapshot artifact with provenance metadata, CI reconciliation test, and CI pipeline configuration.
